claims.claim: python-pptx current stable is 1.0.2 (released 2024-08-07), installed via `pip install python-pptx`, MIT-licensed, Python 3.8+. Version 1.0.x still exposes no high-level RTL API, so RTL must be patched at the XML level. Verified: the bundled create-presentation.py runs cleanly under python-pptx 1.0.2 and produces a valid 5-slide RTL deck.
claims.raw_snippet: python-pptx 1.0.2 is the current stable release (Aug 2024); no newer release as of mid-2026; no native RTL paragraph API added.

claims.claim: Per OOXML (ISO/IEC 29500) DrawingML, right-to-left is the boolean `rtl` ATTRIBUTE on the paragraph-properties elements `<a:pPr>` (CT_TextParagraphProperties) and `<a:lvl1pPr>`..`<a:lvl9pPr>` -- e.g. `<a:pPr algn="r" rtl="1">` -- which is exactly what PowerPoint writes and what LibreOffice and Google Slides import honor. DrawingML has NO `<a:rtl>` element anywhere (datypic e-a_rtl-1.html is 404) and run properties `<a:rPr>` / `<a:defRPr>` (CT_TextCharacterProperties) carry no `rtl` attribute, so there is no run-level direction toggle (unlike WordprocessingML's `<w:rtl/>`). Inline LTR runs inside an RTL paragraph render LTR via the Unicode bidi algorithm; ambiguous boundaries are fixed with bidi controls (LRM U+200E, LRI U+2066 / PDI U+2069) in the text, not XML.
claims.raw_snippet: datypic CT_TextParagraphProperties (a:pPr): rtl [0..1] xsd:boolean 'Right To Left' is an ATTRIBUTE. a:rPr (CT_TextCharacterProperties) lists no rtl attribute and no a:rtl child element. The element page e-a_rtl-1.html returns 404 (no such element in the drawingml namespace).

claims.claim: Marp CLI current release is 4.4.0 (2026-05-06). The export flags `--pdf`, `--html`, `--pptx`, and `--allow-local-files` are current. Default `--pptx` exports each slide as a raster image (RTL baked in visually, text not editable); `--pptx-editable` (Marp CLI 4.1.0+, experimental, via LibreOffice Impress) yields editable text with LibreOffice-dependent RTL fidelity. Marp CLI 4.0+ adds `--browser`, `--browser-path`, `--browser-protocol` flags (and Firefox via WebDriver BiDi), which supersede the legacy PUPPETEER_EXECUTABLE_PATH env var.
claims.raw_snippet: Marp CLI v4.4.0 (May 2026); v4.1.0 added experimental --pptx-editable (LibreOffice-backed); v4.0 added --browser / --browser-path. Core --pdf/--html/--pptx/--allow-local-files flags unchanged.

claims.claim: Marp / Marp Core has no engine-level native RTL support as of CLI 4.4.0; RTL is achieved via a CSS theme (`direction: rtl; text-align: right`) and/or a per-slide `dir: rtl` front-matter / `dir="auto"` attribute. Many built-in themes ignore RTL.
claims.raw_snippet: Marp maintainer confirms no native RTL; recommends CSS direction or a dir-attribute plugin; built-in themes are LTR-oriented.

claims.claim: Heebo (business/tech default), David Libre (formal), Assistant (education), and Rubik (bold/startup) are all current, actively-served Hebrew Google Fonts with full Hebrew subsets in 2026. Heebo is the de-facto default Hebrew sans.
claims.raw_snippet: Heebo, David Libre, Assistant, Rubik are Google Fonts with Hebrew subsets, actively served in 2026.
